# arxiv-radar

> Your daily AI-powered radar for robotics research. arxiv-radar auto-fetches new arXiv papers on a per-user schedule, analyzes each one with an LLM (summary, hypothesis, method, problem, conclusion), auto-tags the topics you care about, and lets you chat with any paper. Every user gets isolated papers, tags, settings, and schedules.

Tech: Next.js 16 · React 19 · TypeScript · Tailwind CSS v4 · PostgreSQL · Conductor SSO · OpenAI-compatible LLM

This file explains how to use arxiv-radar and how to configure it, for both end users (in-app settings) and operators (deployment env vars). UI labels are shown in Chinese because the app interface is in Chinese.

## Getting started (end user)

1. Open the site and sign in with **Conductor SSO** (登录). Login is required — nothing is visible until you are signed in.
2. On your **first login** a setup dialog ("初始化配置") opens automatically. You cannot close it until you fill the required fields and save. This is intentional: the app only becomes usable after it is configured.
3. Fill the required settings (marked with a red `*`), press **完成配置** (Finish setup), and the dashboard becomes available.
4. Wait for the first scheduled run, or trigger an analysis manually, to populate papers.

You can reopen settings any time via the gear icon (⚙️ "配置") in the top-right header.

## In-app configuration (gear menu "配置")

Each field has a `?` icon — hover (or focus) it for inline help. Required fields are marked `*`.

### Section: arxiv daily
- **拉取链接 (Fetch URL)** — REQUIRED. An arXiv listing page URL that decides which papers are pulled each day. Pick a category on arxiv.org and copy the address-bar URL.
  - Example (robotics / cs.RO): `https://arxiv.org/list/cs.RO/recent?skip=0&show=100`

### Section: cron
- **自动拉取 (Auto-fetch)** — toggle. When on, the system fetches and analyzes new papers daily at the time below.
- **time** — daily fetch time in `HH:mm`, 24-hour, interpreted in the server time zone (`APP_TIME_ZONE`, default `Asia/Shanghai`).

### Section: chat (Conductor)
Powers "chat with a paper". The Conductor base URL and token come from your SSO session, so they are not entered here.
- **daemon (daemon host)** — REQUIRED. The Conductor daemon host that runs the AI chat, e.g. `m1`. Provided by your Conductor environment.
- **AI backend** — optional. The backend/model type for paper chat, e.g. `codex` or `claude`. Leave empty to use the server default.
- **workspace** — REQUIRED. Absolute path of the working directory used for chats, e.g. `~/ws/workspace`. It is auto-created on first bind.
- **app name** — defaults to `arxiv-radar-chat`. The Conductor application name used to isolate this app's chat tasks. Usually keep the default.

### Section: tags
- Custom paper tags used for auto-classification and filtering. Each tag has:
  - **ID** — lowercase identifier (e.g. `vla`).
  - **显示名 (Label)** — text shown in the UI (e.g. `VLA`).
- Empty by default; add your own. Click "添加" to add a tag, the trash icon to remove one.

> Minimum required to start using the app: **Fetch URL + daemon + workspace**. (AI backend, app name, and tags are optional.)

## Using the dashboard

- **Paper list** — analyzed papers grouped by date; each card shows the LLM summary, hypothesis, method, problem, conclusion, tags (with evidence), and any detected GitHub link.
- **Filters** — "全部" (all), each tag, "收藏" (favorites), and chat status (running / stopped).
- **Run analysis** — manually trigger a fetch + analysis run for your account.
- **Manual add** — add a specific paper by arXiv id/URL.
- **Chat** — open a paper and ask questions; chats are backed by per-user Conductor tasks.
- **Favorites / hide / custom tags / GitHub link** — per-paper actions, all scoped to your account.
- **PWA** — installable; works well on mobile.

## Deployment configuration (operator, .env.local)

Set these environment variables (see `.env.example`). Env values can also seed defaults for new users' in-app settings.

- `DATABASE_URL` — PostgreSQL connection string.
- `OPENAI_URL` / `OPENAI_API_KEY` / `OPENAI_MODEL` — OpenAI-compatible LLM endpoint used for analysis.
- `APP_URL` — this app's public URL.
- `CONDUCTOR_BASE_URL` — Conductor SSO base URL.
- `CONDUCTOR_SSO_CLIENT_ID` / `CONDUCTOR_SSO_CLIENT_SECRET` — Conductor SSO client credentials.
- `ARXIV_AUTH_SECRET` — secret for encrypting the session cookie.
- `CRON_SECRET` — optional bearer token guarding the cron endpoint.
- `APP_TIME_ZONE` — time zone for cron scheduling (default `Asia/Shanghai`).
- Optional setting seeds (otherwise empty for new users): `ARXIV_DAILY_URL`, `ARXIV_AUTO_FETCH_ENABLED`, `ARXIV_RUN_HOUR`, `ARXIV_RUN_MINUTE`, `CONDUCTOR_DAEMON_HOST`, `CONDUCTOR_WORKSPACE_PATH`, `CONDUCTOR_APP_NAME` (default `arxiv-radar-chat`), `CONDUCTOR_BACKEND_TYPE`.

Register an `arxiv-radar` client on Conductor with `${APP_URL}/api/auth/callback` in its `redirect_uris`.

Setup:
```bash
npm install
cp .env.example .env.local   # fill in keys
npm run db:migrate
npm run dev                  # http://localhost:3000
```

## Scheduled fetching

- `GET /api/cron/arxiv` — automatic run for all users (send `Authorization: Bearer $CRON_SECRET`). Users without a Fetch URL are skipped.
- `POST /api/cron/arxiv?manual=1` — manual run for the logged-in user.
- `npm run cron` — trigger the auto cron once locally.
- `npm run worker` — local worker, polls every 5 minutes.

Use `npm run worker` or an external scheduler to call the cron endpoint. Actual run times come from each user's settings and `APP_TIME_ZONE`.

## Links

- Source & full docs: see the project `README.md`.
